heading: Employee health insurance expenses of small employers
label: 45R Employee health insurance expenses of small employers

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: For purposes of section 38, in the case of an eligible small employer, the small employer health insurance credit determined under this section for any taxable year in the credit period is the amount determined under subsection (b).

the aggregate amount of nonelective contributions the employer made on behalf of its employees during the taxable year under the arrangement described in subsection (d)(4) for premiums for qualified health plans offered by the employer to its employees through an Exchange, or

the aggregate amount of nonelective contributions which the employer would have made during the taxable year under the arrangement if each employee taken into account under paragraph (1) had enrolled in a qualified health plan which had a premium equal to the average premium (as determined by the Secretary of Health and Human Services) for the small group market in the rating area in which the employee enrolls for coverage.

children.heading: Phaseout of credit amount based on number of employees and average wages
children.label: c Phaseout of credit amount based on number of employees and average wages

children.text: Such amount multiplied by a fraction the numerator of which is the total number of full-time equivalent employees of the employer in excess of 10 and the denominator of which is 15.

Such amount multiplied by a fraction the numerator of which is the average annual wages of the employer in excess of the dollar amount in effect under subsection (d)(3)(B) and the denominator of which is such dollar amount.

children.label: A
children.text: which has no more than 25 full-time equivalent employees for the taxable year,

children.label: B
children.text: the average annual wages of which do not exceed an amount equal to twice the dollar amount in effect under paragraph (3)(B) for the taxable year, and

children.label: C
children.text: which has in effect an arrangement described in paragraph (4).

children.label: i
children.text: the total number of hours of service for which wages were paid by the employer to employees during the taxable year, by

children.heading: Excess hours not counted
children.label: B Excess hours not counted
children.text: If an employee works in excess of 2,080 hours of service during any taxable year, such excess shall not be taken into account under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Hours of service
children.label: C Hours of service
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Labor, shall prescribe such regulations, rules, and guidance as may be necessary to determine the hours of service of an employee, including rules for the application of this paragraph to employees who are not compensated on an hourly basis.

children.label: i
children.text: the aggregate amount of wages which were paid by the employer to employees during the taxable year, by

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of full-time equivalent employees of the employee determined under paragraph (2) for the taxable year.

children.heading: 2010, 2011, 2012, and 2013
children.label: i 2010, 2011, 2012, and 2013
children.text: The dollar amount in effect under this paragraph for taxable years beginning in 2010, 2011, 2012, or 2013 is $25,000.

children.heading: Subsequent years
children.label: ii Subsequent years
children.text: In the case of a taxable year beginning in a calendar year after 2013, the dollar amount in effect under this paragraph shall be equal to $25,000, multiplied by the cost-of-living adjustment under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year, determined by substituting “calendar year 2012” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.heading: Contribution arrangement
children.label: 4 Contribution arrangement
children.text: An arrangement is described in this paragraph if it requires an eligible small employer to make a nonelective contribution on behalf of each employee who enrolls in a qualified health plan offered to employees by the employer through an exchange in an amount equal to a uniform percentage (not less than 50 percent) of the premium cost of the qualified health plan.

children.heading: Seasonal worker hours and wages not counted
children.label: 5 Seasonal worker hours and wages not counted

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The number of hours of service worked by, and wages paid to, a seasonal worker of an employer shall not be taken into account in determining the full-time equivalent employees and average annual wages of the employer unless the worker works for the employer on more than 120 days during the taxable year.

children.heading: Definition of seasonal worker
children.label: B Definition of seasonal worker
children.text: The term “seasonal worker” means a worker who performs labor or services on a seasonal basis as defined by the Secretary of Labor, including workers covered by section 500.20(s)(1) of title 29, Code of Federal Regulations and retail workers employed exclusively during holiday seasons.

children.label: i
children.text: an employee within the meaning of section 401(c)(1),

children.label: ii
children.text: any 2-percent shareholder (as defined in section 1372(b)) of an eligible small business which is an S corporation,

children.label: iii
children.text: any 5-percent owner (as defined in section 416(i)(1)(B)(i)) of an eligible small business, or

children.label: iv
children.text: any individual who bears any of the relationships described in subparagraphs (A) through (G) of section 152(d)(2) to, or is a dependent described in section 152(d)(2)(H) of, an individual described in clause (i), (ii), or (iii).

children.heading: Leased employees
children.label: B Leased employees
children.text: The term “employee” shall include a leased employee within the meaning of section 414(n).

children.heading: Credit period
children.label: 2 Credit period
children.text: The term “credit period” means, with respect to any eligible small employer, the 2-consecutive-taxable year period beginning with the 1st taxable year in which the employer (or any predecessor) offers 1 or more qualified health plans to its employees through an Exchange.

children.heading: Nonelective contribution
children.label: 3 Nonelective contribution
children.text: The term “nonelective contribution” means an employer contribution other than an employer contribution pursuant to a salary reduction arrangement.

children.heading: Wages
children.label: 4 Wages
children.text: The term “wages” has the meaning given such term by section 3121(a) (determined without regard to any dollar limitation contained in such section).

children.heading: Aggregation and other rules made applicable
children.label: 5 Aggregation and other rules made applicable

children.heading: Aggregation rules
children.label: A Aggregation rules
children.text: All employers treated as a single employer under subsection (b), (c), (m), or () of section 414 shall be treated as a single employer for purposes of this section.o

children.heading: Other rules
children.label: B Other rules
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of subsections (c), (d), and (e) of section 52 shall apply.

children.heading: Credit made available to tax-exempt eligible small employers
children.label: f Credit made available to tax-exempt eligible small employers
A
children.text: the amount of the credit determined under this section with respect to such employer, or

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of the payroll taxes of the employer during the calendar year in which the taxable year begins.

children.heading: Tax-exempt eligible small employer
children.label: 2 Tax-exempt eligible small employer
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “tax-exempt eligible small employer” means an eligible small employer which is any organization described in section 501(c) which is exempt from taxation under section 501(a).

children.label: i
children.text: amounts required to be withheld from the employees of the tax-exempt eligible small employer under section 3401(a),

children.label: ii
children.text: amounts required to be withheld from such employees under section 3101(b), and

children.label: iii
children.text: amounts of the taxes imposed on the tax-exempt eligible small employer under section 3111(b).

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: B Special rule
children.text: A rule similar to the rule of section 24(d)(2)(C) shall apply for purposes of subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Application of section for calendar years 2010, 2011, 2012, and 2013
children.label: g Application of section for calendar years 2010, 2011, 2012, and 2013

children.heading: No credit period required
children.label: 1 No credit period required
children.text: The credit shall be determined without regard to whether the taxable year is in a credit period and for purposes of applying this section to taxable years beginning after 2013, no credit period shall be treated as beginning with a taxable year beginning before 2014.

children.label: A
children.text: by substituting “35 percent (25 percent in the case of a tax-exempt eligible small employer)” for “50 percent (35 percent in the case of a tax-exempt eligible small employer)”,

children.label: B
children.text: by reference to an eligible small employer’s nonelective contributions for premiums paid for health insurance coverage (within the meaning of section 9832(b)(1)) of an employee, and

children.label: C
children.text: by substituting for the average premium determined under subsection (b)(2) the amount the Secretary of Health and Human Services determines is the average premium for the small group market in the State in which the employer is offering health insurance coverage (or for such area within the State as is specified by the Secretary).

children.heading: Contribution arrangement
children.label: 3 Contribution arrangement
children.text: An arrangement shall not fail to meet the requirements of subsection (d)(4) solely because it provides for the offering of insurance outside of an Exchange.

children.heading: Insurance definitions
children.label: h Insurance definitions
children.text: Any term used in this section which is also used in the Public Health Service Act or subtitle A of title I of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act shall have the meaning given such term by such Act or subtitle.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: i Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this section, including regulations to prevent the avoidance of the 2-year limit on the credit period through the use of successor entities and the avoidance of the limitations under subsection (c) through the use of multiple entities.
